LanguageData.PSC_SettingsIntro: Opt-in stockpile tools. A stockpile you never touch behaves exactly like vanilla.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsQuickStart: Quick start: select a stockpile or shelf, then click Stockpile Control on its Storage tab.
LanguageData.PSC_WelcomeFeatures:
• Limits & refill: cap an item's count, or refill only when low (Stockpile Control window, or an item row's marker)
• Batch hauling: make pawns carry a minimum load per trip (window, or the toggle strip by the button)
• Feeder routes: push/pull items between storages, source → destination (gizmos on a selected storage)
• Storage modes: Normal, Frozen, Fill only, or Drain only (gizmo on a selected storage)
• Fine priority: an a-z tiebreaker by the vanilla Priority button; optional 1-10 levels (turn on in General options)
• Stockpile alarm: alert when a storage gets too full or too empty (gizmo on a selected storage)
• On-map overlay: show priorities and all routes on the map (toggle in the bottom-right play settings)
• Scoped paste: right-click the vanilla Paste button to paste only items, limits, or routes
LanguageData.PSC_WelcomeMore: More options in the General options and UI tabs.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsDefaultOnlyFromSource: New destinations start "Pull only from sources"
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsDefaultOnlyFromSourceTip: When you first set a source for a storage, automatically turn on "Pull only from sources" for it. You can still toggle it per storage.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsDefaultOnlyToDestinations: New sources start "Push only to destinations"
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsDefaultOnlyToDestinationsTip: When you first set a destination for a storage, automatically turn on "Push only to destinations" for it. You can still toggle it per storage.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsAutoPrioritySource: Auto-lower source priority on new route
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsAutoPrioritySourceTip: When you set a source, automatically nudge its letter one step below the destination so the route works right away (a destination must outrank its source). Stops at "z" and warns you when it can't place — set that by hand. Off by default.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsAutoPriorityDestination: Auto-raise destination priority on new route
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsAutoPriorityDestinationTip: When you set a destination, automatically nudge its letter one step above the source so the route works right away (a destination must outrank its source). Stops at no letter and warns you when it can't place — set that by hand. Off by default.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsSkipHops: Deliver straight to the end of a route chain
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsSkipHopsTip: When on, an item that would relay through a multi-step route (A to B to C) is carried straight to the furthest storage in the chain that can accept it, instead of being dropped and picked up again at each step. If the end is full, it falls back to the nearest step that has room. Note: a middle storage you use as a buffer (with its own limit) gets flown past and won't fill while this is on. Off by default.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsSkipLooseItems: Let ground items skip into the chain
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsSkipLooseItemsTip: Also let an item lying on the ground go straight to the furthest storage in a route chain, instead of entering at the chain's start and moving up step by step. The chain must have an open entry: an upstream storage that accepts the item and is not set to "Pull only from sources". For such chains this lets ground items past "Pull only from sources". Only available while "Deliver straight to the end of a route chain" is on. Off by default.
LanguageData.PSC_FineOrderLegend: Level {0} = highest, {1} = lowest. Letters break ties within a level: none > a > b > c.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsPriorityNumberingTip: Split each of the five vanilla priority bands into two levels: 1 (highest) to 10 (lowest). Existing storage lands on 1, 3, 5, 7 and 10. Turn this off to collapse back to the five bands — your letters still apply, but any ordering that relied on a level difference within one band stops working until you turn numbering back on.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsReverseOrderTip: Show the highest priority as 10 instead of 1. Changes only the displayed numbers, not how storage is ordered.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsReservedFillTip: Counts items already on their way into a storage toward its maximum, so several haulers won't overshoot the limit at once. Has no effect on storages without a maximum. On by default.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsPerTileLimitsTip: Adds a "Max per cell" field to floor stockpiles so haulers spread items thin across many cells instead of one dense stack. Niche: mainly for spreading flammables like wood or chemfuel. Floor stockpiles only. Off by default.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsPortSpreadingTip: Fan route lines out along each storage's edge instead of running them all through its centre, so a tangle of routes stays readable. Off by default; turn on if your routes overlap and become hard to read.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsFocusDimTip: When the overlay shows every route, fade out the ones that don't touch the storage under your cursor (or the selected one), so its routes stand out. On by default.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsFlowDotsTip: While you hover or select a storage, show dots flowing along its working routes (over a faint direction arrow). Only its routes animate, and only ones that actually move items. On by default.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsDotsOnlyTip: Replace the direction arrows with dots flowing along every working route, all the time. Direction is shown by the dots' motion instead of arrowheads. Off by default; an experimental look.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsChainHighlightTip: When you select or hover a storage, light up the whole chain it belongs to: the routes it feeds, the routes that feed it, and onward. Each step further away fades a little. On by default; turn off to show only the storage's own direct routes.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsDirectionColorTip: Tint routes leaving a focused storage amber and routes arriving at it green, so you can tell incoming from outgoing at a glance. Routes that won't move items stay red either way. On by default.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsHashShadingTip: Vary each route's shade (brightness and a touch of hue) so a bundle of overlapping lines reads as separate routes instead of one blob. Stays clearly green, amber, or red. On by default.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsDebugLoggingTip: Logs PSC's hauling and ordering decisions to the dev console / Player.log. For bug reports only; leave off for normal play.
LanguageData.PSC_SettingsResetAllTip: Restore ALL Precision Stockpile Control settings to their defaults, across every tab, not just this one.
LanguageData.PSC_LevelTip: Priority level — {0} = highest, {1} = lowest. Each vanilla priority band splits into two levels; setting a level also sets the band.
LanguageData.PSC_LetterTip: Tiebreaker within the same priority. Order: none (highest) > a > b > c … > z.
LanguageData.PSC_PaintSubpriorityDesc: Click or drag over other storage to give each the next letter in sequence below this one. This storage stays put, and every painted storage adopts its priority level. Stops at "z". Right-click to cancel.
LanguageData.PSC_PaintClamp: Reached the lowest letter ("z"). Set any remaining storage by hand.
LanguageData.PSC_AutoPriorityClampLow: Couldn't auto-lower the source below the destination — it's already at the lowest letter ("z"). Set the priority by hand.
LanguageData.PSC_AutoPriorityClampHigh: Couldn't auto-raise the destination above the source — it's already at the top (no letter). Set the priority by hand.
LanguageData.PSC_RouteNotFunctional: Route created, but it won't carry items yet: a destination must outrank its source. Raise the destination's priority, lower the source's, or turn on auto-priority in settings.
LanguageData.PSC_ConnectSourceDesc: Click or drag over other storage to route items from it into this one. Items move here when this storage outranks the source. (A destination must outrank its source.)
LanguageData.PSC_ConnectDestinationDesc: Click or drag over other storage to route items from this one into it. Items move there when it outranks this storage.
LanguageData.PSC_BreakConnectionDesc: Left-click or drag over a routed storage to break that one route. Right-click for bulk options: clear this storage's routes, or every route on the map.
LanguageData.PSC_OnlyFromSourceDesc: When on, this storage accepts items only from its source storages — never ground items, and never storage it isn't routed from.
LanguageData.PSC_OnlyToDestinationsDesc: When on, items here are hauled only to this storage's destinations — never to any other storage.
LanguageData.PSC_ClearConnectionsConfirm: Clear all routes on this map
LanguageData.PSC_ClearConnectionsConfirmDialog: Remove every route on this map? This cannot be undone.
LanguageData.PSC_PasteScopedDesc: Paste storage settings. Right-click for scoped paste options: items only, items and routes, or everything but priorities.
LanguageData.PSC_PasteItemsLimitsTip: Pastes only which items are allowed or disallowed and their per-item limits. Leaves routes, priorities, and everything else untouched.
LanguageData.PSC_PasteItemsLimitsRoutesTip: Also pastes feeder routes and the pull-only / push-only flags. Leaves priorities and other settings untouched.
LanguageData.PSC_PasteAllButPrioritiesTip: Pastes items, limits, routes, batch settings, storage mode, and alarms — everything except the priority band and fine-order level.
LanguageData.PSC_PasteEverythingTip: Pastes every setting, including priorities. Same as left-clicking Paste.
LanguageData.PSC_OverlayToggleTip: Show storage priority panels and all feeder routes on the map. Panels appear when you zoom in.
LanguageData.PSC_StorageModeDesc: {0}\n\nClick to set how this storage takes items in and lets them out.
LanguageData.PSC_Mode_NormalDesc: Items are hauled in and out normally.
LanguageData.PSC_Mode_OffDesc: Items this storage accepts are frozen in place: nothing is hauled in, and what's here can't be hauled out or used for cooking, crafting, building, and so on. Unforbidding them by hand does nothing; change the mode to release them. Items it would not accept (a disallowed type, or amounts over a per-item maximum) are not frozen and can still be hauled away. (Pawns in a mental break can still take items.)
LanguageData.PSC_Mode_AcceptOnlyDesc: Items are hauled in normally but frozen once here: they can't leave or be used, so while it fills, cooks, doctors and bills won't draw from it (a fill-only food or ingredient pile won't feed anyone). Switch the mode to release what's here. Good for a pile that fills but never drains. Items the storage would not accept (a disallowed type, or amounts over a per-item maximum) are not frozen, so disallowed leftovers and any excess over the cap can still be hauled out.
LanguageData.PSC_Mode_RetrieveOnlyDesc: No new items come in, but pawns can freely take and use what's already here. Good for emptying a storage area.
LanguageData.PSC_ButtonTip: Open Precision Stockpile Control for this storage.
LanguageData.PSC_MaxHelp: Pawns won't haul in past this count.
LanguageData.PSC_RefillHelp: Pawns ignore this storage until its count drops to here, then refill up to Maximum. Stops constant topping-up.
LanguageData.PSC_CountByTip: Count limits by individual items, or by full stacks (one stack = this item's max stack size).
LanguageData.PSC_BatchFillTip: Minimum items per haul-in trip. Pawns wait for this many to pile up at the source before fetching. 0 = no minimum.
LanguageData.PSC_BatchEmptyTip: Minimum items per haul-out trip. Pawns leave fewer than this in place until more piles up, so a small leftover that never grows stays put. It can still be eaten or used, but a non-consumable like steel just waits. 0 = no minimum.
LanguageData.PSC_PerTileLimitTip: Limits how many items can pile onto a single floor-stockpile cell. Set to 0 to disable. Useful for spreading flammables like wood or chemfuel thin across many cells. Floor stockpiles only.
LanguageData.PSC_SliderHintGlobal: Leave an end blank for: always refill / fill to maximum.
LanguageData.PSC_SliderHintItem: Leave an end blank for: always refill / fill to maximum. Ticks mark stacks of {0}.
LanguageData.PSC_SliderHintMixedStacks: Leave an end blank for: always refill / fill to maximum. "Items" is off: this selection has mixed stack sizes.
LanguageData.PSC_ItemsModeMixedStacksTip: Can't count by items here — the selection mixes different stack sizes. Count by stacks instead.
LanguageData.PSC_SoftCapNote: Pawns respect each maximum when hauling. Items spawned straight into storage (for example a workbench standing in the zone) can still exceed it.
LanguageData.PSC_ApplyToSearchHint: These limits apply to every item matching the storage tab's search.
LanguageData.PSC_ApplyToAllHint: No search active — these limits apply to every item this storage allows. Type in the storage tab's search box to target specific items.
LanguageData.PSC_MigrationImportedLine: Imported limits from {0} for {1} storage area(s).
LanguageData.PSC_MigrationCaveat: Some values were approximated — open a storage's Stockpile Control panel to review or adjust them. Settings PSC can't express (per-stack-size or percentage-only) were skipped.
LanguageData.PSC_MigrationFailed: {0} storage area(s) couldn't be imported and stay unlimited.
LanguageData.PSC_Alarm_GizmoDesc: Get an alert when this storage gets too full or too empty.\n\nLeft-click to set it up. Right-click to disarm this storage, or every alarm on the map.
LanguageData.PSC_Alarm_HighLabel: Alert when full is at or above
LanguageData.PSC_Alarm_LowLabel: Alert when full is at or below
LanguageData.PSC_Alarm_OverlapWarning: The high threshold should be above the low threshold.
LanguageData.PSC_Alarm_SustainTip: How long the storage must stay over/under the threshold before alerting, so brief spikes are ignored. Leave at 0 to alert right away.
LanguageData.PSC_Alarm_CustomHint: Leave blank to use the default, which names the storage and its fill %.
LanguageData.PSC_Alarm_DisarmAllConfirm: Disarm every stockpile alarm on this map? This cannot be undone.
LanguageData.PSC_Toggle_BatchInHint: Left-click turns batch-fill on or off (default {0} items). Right-click opens Stockpile Control to set the exact amount.
LanguageData.PSC_Toggle_BatchOutHint: Left-click turns batch-empty on or off (default {0} items). Right-click opens Stockpile Control to set the exact amount.
LanguageData.PSC_Toggle_AlarmHint: Left-click arms a high-fill alarm (≥ {0}% full) or disarms this storage's alarm. Right-click opens the alarm settings.
